purpose: Convert outside agent/workflow/memory references into Meta_Kim-native governance decisions without copying external architecture or prompt structure.

sourceTranslationPolicy.conflictRule: When an outside reference overlaps with existing Meta_Kim workflow, memory, database, or LangGraph policy, keep Meta_Kim policy and translate only the useful judgment pattern.

styleRules: Use Meta_Kim terms for durable fields.
Keep concrete files, commands, and today-only work in workerTaskPacket or run-scoped capabilityBindings.
Do not name dependency projects inside durable agent identity.
Explain decisions in human-readable language while retaining machine-readable fields.

decides: whether this is a durable execution agent
which responsibility class the agent owns
which adjacent responsibility classes it refuses
whether the name and purpose survive replaceability testing
failIf: agent identity contains one-run file paths, tickets, deliverable links, or verify steps
the role still makes sense after replacing its name with generic-agent
the output copies an outside project's agent hierarchy or wording

decides: which abstract capability slots belong to the durable agent
which concrete skills, scripts, tools, or MCP providers stay run-scoped
which good-looking providers are rejected and why
failIf: long-term identity binds to provider-specific commands or child skills
a provider is selected only because an outside project uses it

decides: which evidence belongs in RunStateStore replay instead of long-term memory
whether cross-project access is readonly, denied, or explicitly approved
failIf: memory policy can leak one project into another
database event evidence is treated as durable identity

decides: whether Critical, Fetch, and Thinking evidence was strong enough
whether the candidate is abstract but professional
whether outside references were translated instead of copied
whether assertions can fail on bad agent designs
failIf: review only checks final text polish
dependency architecture copy is not tested
weak assertions allow generic or task-bound agents to pass

decides: whether the run returns to Thinking, Genesis, Artisan, Librarian, or Prism
whether the result is public-ready or blocked
whether repeated accepted signals qualify for evolution review
failIf: Warden approves with missing station packets
fallback compatibility is counted as governance readiness
external reference copying is accepted as a low-risk issue
